name: veriphy
description: A three-step agent that reads any LLM response, identifies factual claims, and finds real arXiv papers to back them up. Works with any topic and any LLM.
author: Automorph

capabilities.name: Mark Claims
capabilities.description: Reads every sentence in an LLM response and labels each as a verifiable factual claim or not.

responseText.description: Full LLM response text to analyse

query.description: The original user question — helps focus claim detection on the most relevant sentences

capabilities.name: Find Citations
capabilities.description: Takes a list of claims and searches arXiv for real papers to support each one. No LLM needed. Up to 4 papers are returned per claim, oldest first. Claims with no match are left uncited.

marked.description: Output of mark-claims, optionally filtered to shortlisted candidates

query.description: Original user question — anchors every arXiv search to the right topic

citations.description: Up to 4 arXiv references (Author, Title, arXiv:ID, Year). Empty if no paper was found.

environment.name: OLLAMA_API_KEY
environment.description: API key for the AI model used in Step 1 (claim detection)

environment.name: OLLAMA_BASE_URL
environment.description: Base URL of the AI model endpoint used in Step 1

environment.name: PROXY_PORT
environment.description: Port the proxy server listens on

environment.name: REAL_LLM_BASE_URL
environment.description: Where the proxy forwards your LLM requests

environment.name: REAL_LLM_API_KEY
environment.description: Key to forward to the upstream LLM (leave blank for local Ollama)

environment.name: DEBUG
environment.description: Set to 1 to print detailed logs to the terminal

integration.usage: new VerificationAgent({ container: el, query: 'your question', apiBase: 'https://your-veriphy-url' })
steps: 2. agent.shortlist()        — Step 2: pick the most relevant, distinct claims (no AI)
3. agent.cite()             — Step 3: find arXiv papers for each claim (no AI)
